metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=full driver=ready — UNBLOCKED by PID binding via history.jsonl (antigravity_cwd.go). The conv-id session now resolves its real workspace cwd from ~/.gemini/antigravity-cli/history.jsonl (keyed by conversationId), so DiscoverPID matches the live agy process and binds pid>0. The readyTTL sweep skips ready sessions whose pid>0 process is alive (pid_manager.go:790-794), so the bound session survives a long idle and resumes on the next prompt. Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** `agy`'s interactive REPL stays alive between turns and resumes on the next prompt — the single native `agy` process (1 process ↔ 1 conversation, agent.go:38) outlives the turn and accepts a second prompt that appends to the same `<conv-id>/.system_generated/logs/transcript.jsonl`. No tool calls are needed (≤0 per the scenario), and `parseUserInput` re-opens the turn on the next `USER_INPUT` (parser.go:64).

**daemon=full (UNBLOCKED).** The cell was previously frozen (`daemon=bug`) because the conv-id session never bound a PID: `DiscoverPID` matches an agy process by cwd, but agy records only its sandbox scratch dir in the transcript body (run_command Cwd), so the match never landed and the session stayed pid=0 — and the readyTTL sweep deletes pid=0 ready sessions on idle (pid_manager.go:796). The new `ExtractCWDFromAntigravityHistory` (antigravity_cwd.go), wired into the git adapter's `GetCWDFromTranscript` (adapter.go:154), resolves the REAL launch workspace from the sibling `history.jsonl` keyed by `conversationId`. With a real cwd, `DiscoverPID` (pid.go:33) matches the live agy process and binds pid>0 to the conv-id session. The readyTTL sweep then SKIPS the session while its process is alive: `if snap.pid > 0 { if syscall.Kill(snap.pid, 0) == nil { continue } }` (pid_manager.go:790-794). So the session persists in `ready` past readyTTL and `ready → working` resumes on the next `USER_INPUT`; the terminal `MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE` with no tool_calls settles it back to `ready` (parser.go:120). LIVE-VERIFIED: a bound session stayed ready+bound across an idle window while agy ran.

**driver=ready.** The recipe is two `send`+`wait_turn` turns wrapped around a `sleep` longer than the 15s TTL. All three step types are implemented in `driver-interactive.sh` (`send`→send_text, `wait_turn`→turn-count poll L292, `sleep` L494). Only `interrupt`/`reset_session` are stubbed, and neither is used here.
caveats: Antigravity emits no explicit end-of-turn marker; the daemon infers turn_done from a terminal MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE with no tool_calls (parser.go:120). The no-tool prompts make this inference unambiguous, but the ready-settle remains provisional on emission until the live recording confirms it.

Recording uses IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s so a ~20s idle window cleanly exceeds the TTL; the spec asserts duration_at_least_ms (a floor), so production 30-min idles are by definition still passing.

PID binding depends on history.jsonl carrying the conv-id's workspace entry; the index is written lazily and the resolver retries on the next activity refresh (antigravity_cwd.go doc). The recording must confirm the bind landed before the idle window.
observations omit cost/tokens — antigravity's JSONL carries no usage (tokens live in the per-conversation SQLite protobuf store the parser does not read).

sources.note: ExtractCWDFromAntigravityHistory resolves the real workspace cwd from history.jsonl keyed by conversationId — the bind that flips this cell
GetCWDFromTranscript calls ExtractCWDFromAntigravityHistory — the wiring point
readyTTL sweep SKIPS a ready session whose pid>0 process is alive (syscall.Kill(pid,0)==nil → continue) — the liveness hold
USER_INPUT re-opens the turn → working on the next prompt; terminal PLANNER_RESPONSE no-tool → turn_done → ready

preconditions: agy CLI on PATH (1.0.10), Google OAuth authenticated; no API key
irrlichd running with IRRLICHT_READY_SESSION_TTL=15s (default 30 min would force a 32+ min recording)
irrlichd in --record mode with grant-all permission mode (transcripts permission)
history.jsonl must carry the conv-id's workspace entry so the conv-id session binds pid>0 before the idle window
setup: Driver launches agy under tmux in a fresh, real workspace cwd; ExtractCWDFromAntigravityHistory resolves that cwd → DiscoverPID binds the agy PID to the conv-id session.
The post-turn sleep (20s) is ~1.33x the 15s readyTTL — long enough to prove 'past TTL'.

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: alive

verify: the agy session binds pid>0 (cwd resolved via history.jsonl) and enters ready after turn 1
no transcript_removed for the session during the 20s idle window (not swept while agy is alive)
no state_transition to working/waiting during the idle window (state stays ready)
after the second send: ready → working → ready for the SAME session
